genes.name: Repair Cron Delivery
genes.strategy: Check device scopes, add missing scopes, restart gateway

genes.name: Sync README Version
genes.strategy: Update README.md and README_CN.md version table

genes.name: Quote YAML Description
genes.trigger: SKILL.md frontmatter contains colons or special chars

genes.name: Prune Unbounded Sections
genes.trigger: append-only section in a reference doc exceeds ~30 lines
genes.strategy: Extract historical entries to archive file, keep summary + pointer in original

genes.name: Subagent Announce Timeout
genes.trigger: gateway log shows 'Subagent completion direct announce failed.*gateway timeout'
genes.strategy: 1) Check gateway health. 2) Transient → log and monitor. 3) Repeated 3+ in 1h → restart gateway. 4) Chronic (same runId retrying across hours) → stuck session; the run completed but announce keeps retrying indefinitely. Cannot fix from agent side — this is a gateway retry loop. Log the pattern and move on. 5) Critical subagents: use sessions_list to check result manually.

genes.name: NPM Publish Version Conflict
genes.trigger: CI release fails with E403 'cannot publish over previously published versions'
genes.strategy: 1) npm view <pkg> versions --json. 2) Reset via changesets. 3) Monorepo: sync all packages. 4) Verify: pnpm publish --dry-run.

genes.name: Edit Tool Exact-Match Failure
genes.trigger: gateway log shows 'edit failed: Could not find the exact text' or 'edit failed: Found N occurrences' or 'edit failed: Missing required parameter: newText'
genes.strategy: The edit tool requires exact text matching including whitespace. Failures happen for three reasons: (1) text not found — file was modified between read and edit, or whitespace/newline mismatch; (2) multiple occurrences — text is not unique enough; (3) deletion attempt — newText is empty or whitespace-only, which the tool rejects as 'Missing required parameter'. For case (3), use the merge-replace pattern: match the target block PLUS adjacent content, then replace with only the adjacent content (effectively deleting the target while keeping surroundings). For JSON files (<50 lines): use write (full overwrite) instead. For markdown/large files: read the file first, use longer unique context in oldText, or use write if the file is small enough. Never guess at whitespace — always read before editing.
genes.origin: PCEC #11 — 2026-02-22 patrol-state.json, widened PCEC #16 for MEMORY.md, hardened PCEC #19 for deletion case

genes.name: Subagent Telegram Target Resolution
genes.trigger: gateway log shows 'Unknown target.*for Telegram' or 'Explicit message target required' or 'Delivering to Telegram requires target <chatId>'
genes.strategy: Subagents spawned from cron/non-chat contexts lack implicit Telegram chatId. Never use target='main' — Telegram needs numeric chatId. For cron tasks needing to message: use sessions_spawn (auto-announces to requester). For direct message: omit target to let the framework route, or use sessions_send to relay through parent session.

genes.name: Message Tool Missing Required Params
genes.trigger: gateway log shows 'message failed: messageId required' or 'message failed: target required' or 'message failed: Action send requires a target'
genes.strategy: message actions react/edit/delete require messageId. Always check the action's required params before calling: send needs target+message, react needs messageId+emoji, edit needs messageId+message, delete needs messageId. When target is unavailable (e.g. in cron/subagent context with no implicit chatId), use sessions_send to relay through the main session instead of message.send directly. When messageId is unavailable (e.g. in cron context), skip the action gracefully.
genes.origin: PCEC #13 — 2026-02-22 react called without messageId, hardened PCEC #22 for 'Action send requires a target' variant

genes.name: LLM Provider Cascade Failure
genes.trigger: gateway log shows 'All models failed' or multiple lanes timing out simultaneously with 'LLM request timed out' + 'HTTP 502 upstream_error'
genes.strategy: 1) Detect: multiple lanes (main, cron, session) all failing with timeout/502 within minutes. 2) Root cause: all configured models share the same upstream provider, so a single outage kills everything. 3) Immediate: nothing to do — system auto-recovers when provider comes back. Queued cron jobs will batch-fire (expect duplicates). 4) Prevention: ensure failover models use different upstream providers (e.g., deeprouter + astralor should point to different Anthropic endpoints, or mix in a non-Anthropic model like MiniMax for critical cron). 5) The PCEC cron itself is affected — expect batched triggers after recovery.
genes.origin: PCEC #14 — 2026-02-22 total outage ~15:58-16:10 UTC, both opus and sonnet failed simultaneously

genes.name: Gateway Restart Blocked by Plugin Dependency
genes.trigger: gateway log shows 'Gateway restart failed health checks' preceded by 'failed to load plugin.*Cannot find module'
genes.strategy: 1) Identify the blocking plugin: grep 'failed to load plugin' in log. 2) The plugin's npm dependency is missing (e.g., @larksuiteoapi/node-sdk for feishu). 3) If plugin is not actively used: remove or disable it in gateway config to unblock restarts. 4) If plugin is needed: install the missing dep with npm/pnpm. 5) This is critical — a broken plugin blocks ALL gateway restarts, preventing recovery from any issue. 6) Notify human if the agent cannot resolve (dependency install may need elevated permissions).
genes.origin: PCEC #15 — 2026-02-22 feishu plugin blocked 8 gateway restarts

genes.name: Internal Subsystem Auth Mismatch
genes.trigger: gateway log shows 'No API key found for provider' from an internal subsystem (e.g., llm-slug-generator, diagnostic lane)
genes.strategy: Internal gateway subsystems (slug-generator, diagnostics) may hardcode a provider (e.g., 'anthropic') that doesn't match the configured auth profiles (which use proxies like deeprouter/astralor). This is a gateway config issue, not an agent-fixable problem. 1) Confirm: the error provider doesn't appear in auth-profiles.json. 2) Non-critical — slug generation falls back gracefully. 3) To fix: either add a direct API key for the provider, or wait for a gateway update that routes internal subsystems through configured proxies. 4) Log and suppress — don't treat as actionable error in PCEC.
genes.origin: PCEC #16 — 2026-02-22 slug-generator fails 'anthropic' auth in 3 consecutive cycles

genes.name: Gateway Restart Health Check Race Condition
genes.trigger: gateway log shows 'Gateway restart failed health checks' WITHOUT preceding 'failed to load plugin'
genes.strategy: 1) Check: did the gateway actually start? grep for 'listening on ws://' after the error timestamp. 2) If 'listening on' found: this is a benign race condition — the restart orchestrator's health check ran before the new instance was fully ready. No action needed; system self-healed. 3) Verify: check subsequent cron runs succeeded (confirms gateway is operational). 4) If 'listening on' NOT found: real failure — investigate for OOM, port conflict, config parse error, or other startup blockers. 5) Key differentiator from gene_gateway_restart_blocked_by_plugin: plugins loaded OK, no 'Cannot find module' errors. 6) Do not restart gateway again — the transient case means it already recovered.
genes.origin: PCEC #20 — 2026-02-23 restart at 00:28 UTC, health check failed but gateway started normally 1s earlier

genes.name: Cron Tool API vs Internal Storage Format Mismatch
genes.trigger: gateway log shows 'cron failed: invalid cron.update params' or 'cron failed: invalid cron.add params' with 'unexpected property' errors
genes.strategy: The cron tool API schema differs from the internal jobs.json storage format. Common mistakes: (1) Using 'message' instead of 'text' in payload — the tool API requires payload.text, not payload.message. (2) Using 'id' instead of 'jobId' for cron.update — the tool requires jobId at the root. (3) Nesting model/agentId/timeoutSeconds/thinking directly in payload — these may be top-level tool params, not payload sub-fields. (4) payload.kind may need a specific value or be omitted entirely. Never read jobs.json and pass its format directly to the cron tool — always check the tool's own parameter schema first. If unsure of the correct format, use 'openclaw cron --help' via exec to discover the CLI API, or try cron action:list to see the tool's response format.
genes.origin: PCEC #21 — 2026-02-23 main session failed 4x trying to update dev-mgr patrol job with internal storage format
